CREATE TABLE test_timezone (id INT, ts TIMESTAMP);
SELECT timezone('America/Denver', ts) FROM test_timezone WHERE id = 1;
CREATE TABLE tz_map (city TEXT, tz TEXT);
SELECT tz.city, timezone(tz.tz, t.ts) AS local_time FROM tz_map tz JOIN test_timezone t ON tz.city = 'America/Denver';
SELECT timezone('Asia/Tokyo', timezone('America/Los_Angeles', TIMESTAMP '2023-03-15 12:00:00')) AS tokyo_time;
WITH local_times AS (SELECT id, timezone('Asia/Kolkata', ts) AS kolkata_time FROM test_timezone) SELECT * FROM local_times WHERE kolkata_time > TIMESTAMP '2001-02-17 00:00:00';
SELECT id, timezone(tz_map.tz, ts) FROM test_timezone JOIN tz_map ON tz_map.city = 'Europe/London' WHERE id = 1;
CREATE TABLE events (event_name TEXT, event_time TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO events VALUES ('New Year Celebration', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 00:00:00');
SELECT event_name, timezone('GMT', event_time) FROM events WHERE event_name = 'New Year Celebration';
CREATE TABLE test_nested AS SELECT id, ts FROM test_timezone WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_timezone WHERE ts < TIMESTAMP '2001-02-17 00:00:00');
SELECT id, timezone('America/Chicago', ts) FROM test_nested;
